title: image build does not create exposed /udp port if /tcp is also exposed
body:
### Issue Description

I noticed that `skopeo inspect --config --no-tags docker://quay.io/rhel-primitives/caddy|grep -A5 Exposed` is missing the expected "443/udp" entry from its [Containerfile](https://gitlab.com/redhat/rhel-primitives/containers/-/blob/eafd759806127b3/images/caddy/Containerfile#L54). Note that this already has `EXPOSE 443/tcp`, i.e. for the same port.

This has nothing to do with quay.io or `skopeo`, this affects a locally built podman image as well.

This actually works fine with e.g. `53/udp`, i.e. for a port which isn't already exposed as TCP.

If that isn't a sensible thing to do (I can very well imagine that blocking/forwarding port can't be done by protocol), then it should at least be consistent, or preferably it should drop the `/protocol` suffix altogether on inspection.

Thanks!

### Steps to reproduce the issue

```
printf 'FROM scratch\nEXPOSE 80\nEXPOSE 443/tcp\nEXPOSE 443/udp\n' | podman build -t udp-expose-test -f - . 
podman image inspect udp-expose-test | grep -A10 ExposedPorts
podman rmi localhost/udp-expose-test
```

### Describe the results you received

It shows the TCP port for 443:
```json
               "ExposedPorts": {
                    "443/tcp": {},
                    "80/tcp": {}
               },
```

### Describe the results you expected

It should show `443/udp` as well.

### podman info output

comments.body:
> I noticed that skopeo inspect --config --no-tags docker://quay.io/rhel-primitives/caddy|grep -A5 Exposed is missing the expected "443/udp" entry from its [Containerfile](https://gitlab.com/redhat/rhel-primitives/containers/-/blob/eafd759806127b3/images/caddy/Containerfile#L54). Note that this already has EXPOSE 443/tcp, i.e. for the same port.

So this seems to be an issue with building then not the image inspection on the podman side then I guess.

Ah, you are right -- it works for an existing image which wasn't built with podman:
```
❱❱❱ skopeo inspect --config --no-tags docker://docker.io/caddy|grep -A5 Exposed
        "ExposedPorts": {
            "2019/tcp": {},
            "443/tcp": {},
            "443/udp": {},
            "80/tcp": {}
        },
```

So re titling accordingly.
Aw, neat, you found a bug in imagebuilder.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
This was fixed in https://github.com/openshift/imagebuilder/pull/311 and should find its way into the next buildah/podman release
